forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS36KNHH9BPC82MFMGTY3T5J
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8f80
+project: command-service
+title: '`app` MCP server (replaces app-level Tauri commands)'
+---
+## What
+
+Create a new in-process MCP server `app` exposing app-shell operations (quit, about, help, undo, redo, dismiss). Replaces the app-related `#[tauri::command]` handlers in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs`.
+
+Files:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/Cargo.toml` — new workspace member
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/lib.rs`
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/operations.rs` — operations matching today's Tauri commands: `QuitApp`, `ShowAbout`, `ShowHelp`, `DismissModal`, `Undo`, `Redo`, etc.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/service.rs` — service holding any needed state references (undo manager, modal coordinator)
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/setup.rs` — `host.expose_rust_module("app", AppService::new(...))`
+
+NOTE: `mcp_call` and `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs` are NOT migrated — they ARE the MCP transport between the frontend and Rust. They stay as Tauri commands (per the user's design answer: "internal helpers as Tauri" is the practical exception for the transport itself).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Every non-transport Tauri command in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs` has a corresponding `#[operation]` in `swissarmyhammer-app-service`
+- [ ] Bootstrap registers `app` as an in-process server
+- [ ] Undo/redo round-trips through the unified undo stack (see project `single-changelog`)
+- [ ] No behavior regression vs. today's Tauri implementation
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using real tauri test app
+- [ ] Undo/redo integration test: mutate state via a Command service execute; call `app.undo`; assert state reverted; call `app.redo`; assert state restored
+- [ ] `_meta` snapshot
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-app-service` passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -10,28 +10,37 @@
 ---
 ## What

-Create a new in-process MCP server `app` exposing app-shell operations (quit, about, help, undo, redo, dismiss). Replaces the app-related `#[tauri::command]` handlers in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs`.
+Create a new in-process MCP server `app` exposing app-shell operations: quit, about, help, dismiss, and the **cross-cutting undo/redo**. Replaces the app-related `#[tauri::command]` handlers in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs` and exposes the `StoreContext` undo kernel.

 Files:
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/Cargo.toml` — new workspace member
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/lib.rs`
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/operations.rs` — operations matching today's Tauri commands: `QuitApp`, `ShowAbout`, `ShowHelp`, `DismissModal`, `Undo`, `Redo`, etc.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/service.rs` — service holding any needed state references (undo manager, modal coordinator)
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/setup.rs` — `host.expose_rust_module("app", AppService::new(...))`
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/operations.rs` — operations: `QuitApp`, `ShowAbout`, `ShowHelp`, `Undo`, `Redo`. (NOTE: `app.command`, `app.palette`, `app.search`, `app.dismiss` are UI-state toggles → they go to the `ui-state` server, NOT here. Only true app-shell actions + undo/redo live here.)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/service.rs` — service holding the `StoreContext` reference (the undo kernel)
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/setup.rs` — `host.expose_rust_module("app", AppService::new(store_ctx.clone(), ...))`
+
+### Undo/redo is cross-cutting (key design point)
+
+`app.undo` / `app.redo` operate on the **single unified changelog** (`StoreContext`, the `single-changelog` kernel) — NOT a per-server undo stack. Every `undoable: true` command across every server (entity CRUD via `kanban`, perspective/view mutations via `views`, clipboard/archive via `kanban`) writes through that one changelog. `app.undo` reverts the most recent changelog entry regardless of which command or server produced it. This is why undo lives in one place (`app`) even though it affects entities, views, and perspectives alike. The `app` server does not know about entity types — it just calls `store.undo()` / `store.redo()` on the shared kernel.
+
+Today the undo stack is `StoreContext` (file-backed at `.swissarmyhammer/.undo/` JSONL), reached via `ctx.require_extension::<StoreContext>()`; it has no MCP surface. This task exposes it.

-NOTE: `mcp_call` and `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs` are NOT migrated — they ARE the MCP transport between the frontend and Rust. They stay as Tauri commands (per the user's design answer: "internal helpers as Tauri" is the practical exception for the transport itself).
+NOTE: `mcp_call` / `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs`) are NOT migrated — they ARE the MCP transport. They stay Tauri.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Every non-transport Tauri command in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs` has a corresponding `#[operation]` in `swissarmyhammer-app-service`
-- [ ] Bootstrap registers `app` as an in-process server
-- [ ] Undo/redo round-trips through the unified undo stack (see project `single-changelog`)
+- [ ] `app` registered as an in-process server at bootstrap
+- [ ] `app.quit`, `app.about`, `app.help` route through this server (replacing the Tauri handlers)
+- [ ] `Undo`/`Redo` operate on the shared `StoreContext` changelog
+- [ ] Undo reverts mutations made by ANY server: a kanban entity edit, a views perspective change, and a clipboard paste are each undoable through `app.undo`
 - [ ] No behavior regression vs. today's Tauri implementation

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using real tauri test app
-- [ ] Undo/redo integration test: mutate state via a Command service execute; call `app.undo`; assert state reverted; call `app.redo`; assert state restored
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using a real tauri test app
+- [ ] Cross-cutting undo test: (a) edit a task via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (b) set a perspective filter via `views` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (c) paste via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted. One unified stack reverts all three in LIFO order.
 - [ ] `_meta` snapshot
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-app-service` passes

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd`
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the cross-cutting undo test first; it pins the single-changelog contract.
+
+Depends on the operation-struct foundation. Coordinates with the `single-changelog` project (the unified changelog is the substrate undo relies on).
\ No newline at end of file

 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/Cargo.toml` — new workspace member

+NOTE: `mcp_call` and `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs` are NOT migrated — they ARE the MCP transport between the frontend and Rust. They stay as Tauri commands (per the user's design answer: "internal helpers as Tauri" is the practical exception for the transport itself).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `app` registered as an in-process server at bootstrap
-- [ ] `app.quit`, `app.about`, `app.help` route through this server (replacing the Tauri handlers)
-- [ ] `Undo`/`Redo` operate on the shared `StoreContext` changelog
-- [ ] Undo reverts mutations made by ANY server: a kanban entity edit, a views perspective change, and a clipboard paste are each undoable through `app.undo`
+- [ ] Every non-transport Tauri command in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs` has a corresponding `#[operation]` in `swissarmyhammer-app-service`
+- [ ] Bootstrap registers `app` as an in-process server
+- [ ] Undo/redo round-trips through the unified undo stack (see project `single-changelog`)
 - [ ] No behavior regression vs. today's Tauri implementation

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using a real tauri test app
-- [ ] Cross-cutting undo test: (a) edit a task via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (b) set a perspective filter via `views` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (c) paste via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted. One unified stack reverts all three in LIFO order.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using real tauri test app
+- [ ] Undo/redo integration test: mutate state via a Command service execute; call `app.undo`; assert state reverted; call `app.redo`; assert state restored
 - [ ] `_meta` snapshot
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-app-service` passes

-- Use `/tdd` — write the cross-cutting undo test first; it pins the single-changelog contract.
-
-Depends on the operation-struct foundation. Coordinates with the `single-changelog` project (the unified changelog is the substrate undo relies on).
\ No newline at end of file

 position_ordinal: 8f80
 project: command-service

 ---
 ## What

-Create a new in-process MCP server `app` exposing app-shell operations: quit, about, help, dismiss, and the **cross-cutting undo/redo**. Replaces the app-related `#[tauri::command]` handlers in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs` and exposes the `StoreContext` undo kernel.
+Create a new in-process MCP server `app` exposing genuine app-shell actions: quit, about, help. Replaces the app-shell `#[tauri::command]` handlers in `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/application.rs`.
+
+NOTE: undo/redo are NOT here. They are store-layer concerns and live on the `store` MCP server (see the `store` server task), which exposes them over the shared `StoreContext`. The `app.undo`/`app.redo` *commands* route to `store.undo`/`store.redo`. Likewise `app.command/palette/search/dismiss` are UI-state toggles → `ui_state` server. This `app` server is only the true shell actions.

 Files:
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/Cargo.toml` — new workspace member
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/lib.rs`
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/operations.rs` — operations: `QuitApp`, `ShowAbout`, `ShowHelp`, `Undo`, `Redo`. (NOTE: `app.command`, `app.palette`, `app.search`, `app.dismiss` are UI-state toggles → they go to the `ui-state` server, NOT here. Only true app-shell actions + undo/redo live here.)
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/service.rs` — service holding the `StoreContext` reference (the undo kernel)
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/setup.rs` — `host.expose_rust_module("app", AppService::new(store_ctx.clone(), ...))`
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/operations.rs` — operations: `QuitApp`, `ShowAbout`, `ShowHelp`
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/src/service.rs`
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/setup.rs` — `host.expose_rust_module("app", AppService::new(app_handle))`

-### Undo/redo is cross-cutting (key design point)
-
-`app.undo` / `app.redo` operate on the **single unified changelog** (`StoreContext`, the `single-changelog` kernel) — NOT a per-server undo stack. Every `undoable: true` command across every server (entity CRUD via `kanban`, perspective/view mutations via `views`, clipboard/archive via `kanban`) writes through that one changelog. `app.undo` reverts the most recent changelog entry regardless of which command or server produced it. This is why undo lives in one place (`app`) even though it affects entities, views, and perspectives alike. The `app` server does not know about entity types — it just calls `store.undo()` / `store.redo()` on the shared kernel.
-
-Today the undo stack is `StoreContext` (file-backed at `.swissarmyhammer/.undo/` JSONL), reached via `ctx.require_extension::<StoreContext>()`; it has no MCP surface. This task exposes it.
-
-NOTE: `mcp_call` / `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs`) are NOT migrated — they ARE the MCP transport. They stay Tauri.
+`mcp_call` / `mcp_subscribe` Tauri commands stay Tauri (they are the MCP transport).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `app` registered as an in-process server at bootstrap
 - [ ] `app.quit`, `app.about`, `app.help` route through this server (replacing the Tauri handlers)
-- [ ] `Undo`/`Redo` operate on the shared `StoreContext` changelog
-- [ ] Undo reverts mutations made by ANY server: a kanban entity edit, a views perspective change, and a clipboard paste are each undoable through `app.undo`
-- [ ] No behavior regression vs. today's Tauri implementation
+- [ ] No undo/redo logic in this server (it lives in `store`)
+- [ ] `_meta` tree complete; no behavior regression

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using a real tauri test app
-- [ ] Cross-cutting undo test: (a) edit a task via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (b) set a perspective filter via `views` → `app.undo` → assert reverted; (c) paste via `kanban` → `app.undo` → assert reverted. One unified stack reverts all three in LIFO order.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-app-service/tests/integration/app_e2e.rs` — per-operation tests using a real tauri test app (quit/about/help trigger the expected shell behavior)
 - [ ] `_meta` snapshot
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-app-service` passes

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the cross-cutting undo test first; it pins the single-changelog contract.
+- Use `/tdd`

-Depends on the operation-struct foundation. Coordinates with the `single-changelog` project (the unified changelog is the substrate undo relies on).
\ No newline at end of file
+Depends on the operation-struct foundation. (Cross-cutting undo/redo moved to the `store` server task.)
\ No newline at end of file
